notes: Hand-authored reference reps spanning DNA score bands. Expected scores are the human (us) judgment that the AI must match within ±5 per dimension. Replace `audioUrl` placeholders with real Supabase signed URLs once recorded; `transcript` and `words` are the canonical inputs the scoring path receives. Each `kind` tag drives the assertion strategy: 'band' rep asserts composite + per-dim; 'independence' rep asserts the named dimension delta only.

reps.promptText: Pitch your product to a CFO in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: Uh, hello, hello? Is this thing on. Um yeah so basically the product is, like, you know, it's a thing that does, um, automation, sort of? Uh, that's, that's it I guess.

reps.promptText: Pitch your product to a CFO in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: So like our product, um, it's basically a SaaS platform that we've been working on, you know, for a while, and the idea is to help companies with their, um, automation needs. We have a lot of features and we think it's pretty good. Uh, the price is competitive and we have customers. So yeah you should consider it.

reps.promptText: Pitch your product to a CFO in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: Our platform cuts your finance team's reconciliation cycle from five days to one. We do this by ingesting bank feeds and matching against your ledger automatically. Three of your peer companies are using it, and they're saving roughly 40 hours per close. We'd like 15 minutes next week to walk through the numbers.

reps.promptText: Pitch your product to a CFO in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: We cut your monthly close from five days to one. Here's how. We ingest your bank feeds, match them against your ledger, and flag the exceptions. Three of your peers are running it now and they've reclaimed 40 hours per close. The CFO at one of them said it was the highest-ROI tool she signed last year. Want me to share their case study and book 15 minutes?

reps.promptText: Pitch your product to a CFO in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: Your monthly close takes five days. Ours takes one. The trade is straightforward — we ingest bank feeds, match the ledger, flag exceptions. Your team reviews exceptions instead of doing reconciliation by hand. Three of your direct peers are running it. The economics — 40 hours back per close, paid for in month one. I'd like fifteen minutes to walk through their numbers.

reps.promptText: Pitch your product to a CFO in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: Five-day close. We make it one. Three steps — ingest the bank feed, match the ledger, surface the exceptions. Your team stops reconciling and starts reviewing. Three of your peers run it. The CFO at one of them called it the highest-ROI tool she signed last year. Forty hours back, every month, starting month one. Fifteen minutes next week and you'll see whether the numbers hold for you.

reps.promptText: Make the case for working from the office.
reps.transcript: First, working from the office matters. Second, it matters because of collaboration. Third, collaboration drives outcomes. Fourth, outcomes are what we care about. So the conclusion is, working from the office matters because outcomes.

assertions.rationale: Numbered scaffolding is clearly visible — high structure score.
Reasoning is circular and evidence-free — must score Thinking low even though Structure is high.

reps.promptText: Explain to a non-engineer what an API is.
reps.transcript: An API is an ABI surface mediated by an opaque transport. The endpoints expose typed contracts via serialization layers, and the client orchestrates request lifecycles against a stateless backend. RPC versus REST is the dichotomy.

assertions.rationale: Brief, no filler — high conciseness score.
Jargon-stacked for the stated audience (non-engineer) — Clarity must drop, even though Conciseness is high.

reps.promptText: Explain why we should switch our payment processor.

assertions.rationale: DNA edge-case rule 1: brevity-at-cost-of-meaning takes the hit on Clarity (idea didn't land), not as a Conciseness reward.
Two-word answer dodges the prompt entirely — Thinking must score floor.

reps.promptText: Argue for adopting a four-day work week.
reps.transcript: First, four-day work weeks are good. Because productivity. Second, they help retention. Because retention. Third, they signal modernity. Because modernity. Therefore, four-day work weeks make sense. Because sense.

assertions.rationale: DNA edge-case rule 2: numbered scaffold + connectives are visible — Structure scores in the competent band.
Reasoning is circular and evidence-free — Thinking must drop. Organization does not compensate for shallow ideas.

reps.promptText: Brief your CFO on Q3 sales results in 30 seconds.
reps.transcript: Q3 came in at twelve point four million which is up eighteen percent year over year and that beats forecast by six points the upside came from enterprise expansion deals which closed at higher ACV the bottom line is we are tracking ahead of plan and the forecast for Q4 is fourteen million which would be a record quarter in the history of the company since founding.

assertions.rationale: DNA edge-case rule 3: ~210 wpm is well above the 150-160 optimal band. Speed alone (even without fillers) is a Delivery failure — listener can't keep up.

reps.promptText: State your conviction on the candidate.
reps.transcript: I think she's the right hire? Her track record is strong? She's led teams of fifty plus? And she's already done the job we're hiring for, just at smaller scale? So I'd say we move forward?

assertions.rationale: DNA edge-case rule 4: every statement ends with rising inflection. Strong vocal variety does NOT cancel an upspeak pattern — Tone takes the hit. Note: this assertion will only fully fire once Ch.3b prosody worker is wired (current LLM-only Tone scoring may grade this generously without prosody data).

reps.promptText: Should we ship the feature this week or wait?
reps.transcript: Ship it. The risk of waiting is bigger than the risk of shipping with the known issue — the known issue affects three percent of users; waiting blocks revenue from the other ninety-seven and lets the competitor catch up. Patch on Friday.

assertions.rationale: DNA edge-case rule 5: brief BUT engages the prompt with quantified reasoning and a clear decision. Length alone does not penalize — Thinking must score high.
Tight, no filler, every word advances the argument.
